response: An important leader of the Democratic-Republican Party was Thomas Jefferson, who formed the party in 1791–93 to oppose the centralizing policies of the Federalist Party. He served as a key figure in the party, and later, James Madison also played a significant role as a leader of the party. Additionally, Albert Gallatin, a prominent Swiss-American politician and diplomat, was an important leader of the Democratic-Republican Party, serving as the longest-tenured Secretary of the Treasury.
